title: I Will Build My Church
confidence_notes: Anniversary/church-plant sermon centered on Jesus' promise to build his church, grounding confidence in his identity, cross-and-resurrection work, and the shared call to follow him together.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Scripture
confidence_notes: Doctrine of Scripture sermon: canon, inspiration, authority, sufficiency, Christ-centered revelation, Scripture's saving purpose, and its role in training believers for transformation and good works.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Humanity
confidence_notes: Humanity/image-of-God sermon fits the creation anchor: humans as creatures, embodied image bearers, male and female, made to reflect God's glory, cultivate creation, and have the image restored in Christ.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Sin
confidence_notes: Doctrine of sin sermon treats sin as action, desire, and fallen nature in Adam, then contrasts that with Christ's representative obedience, justification, salvation, repentance, and ongoing war against sin.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Jesus
confidence_notes: Jesus doctrine sermon emphasizes the incarnate Word as fully God and fully human, Son from the Father, center of Scripture, crucified and raised, and personally received by faith for life.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About the Holy Spirit
confidence_notes: Holy Spirit sermon on Pentecost, the Spirit's deity/personhood, indwelling presence, regeneration, transformation, gifts for the church, witness to Christ, and communion as fellowship by the Spirit.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About the Church
confidence_notes: First church sermon focuses on identity: the church as community, family, temple, and kingdom outpost, created for fellowship, gathered by the gospel, indwelt by the Spirit, and witnessing to Christ's reign.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About the Church
confidence_notes: Second church sermon focuses on practices: receiving the gospel, baptism, apostles' teaching, fellowship, breaking bread, prayers, shared life in homes, generosity, and common witness as the Lord adds to the church.

title: Joyful Surrender
confidence_notes: Advent joy sermon on the treasure hidden in the field: seeing Christ and his kingdom as supremely valuable, joyfully surrendering lesser treasures, and letting that joy reshape giving, obedience, and worship.

title: Why Bethlehem?
confidence_notes: Advent/Christmas sermon asks why Bethlehem: fulfilled prophecy, the birth of the eternal King, sorrow and death answered by Christ's death and victory, hope beyond death, and the call to receive the Savior.
